nodes.x: Hear the right, O Lord, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.
Give ear, O Lord, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;
Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.
Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.
But behold, verily, verily, I say unto you that mine eyes are upon you. I am in your midst and ye cannot see me;
Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.
Now, my God, let, I beseech thee, thine eyes be open, and let thine ears be attent unto the prayer that is made in this place.
Thou hast proved mine heart; thou hast visited me in the night; thou hast tried me, and shalt find nothing; I am purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
¶ For thou hast trusted in thy wickedness: thou hast said, None seeth me. Thy wisdom and thy knowledge, it hath perverted thee; and thou hast said in thine heart, I am, and none else beside me.
* 1. Thou hast sought after me in Babylon ; thou hast brought me out from the pride of priestly power ; from a deceitful and corrupt generation, even from the wilds of sin, and the wages of error.
But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.
My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept me from the paths of the destroyer.
For thou, Lord, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.
Keep me, O Lord, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from the violent man; who have purposed to overthrow my goings.

And, behold, one like the similitude of the sons of men touched my lips: then I opened my mouth, and spake, and said unto him that stood before me, O my lord, by the vision my sorrows are turned upon me, and I have retained no strength.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.
Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O Lord, held me up.
My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
Moreover he said unto me, Son of man, all my words that I shall speak unto thee receive in thine heart, and hear with thine ears.
Nevertheless hear thou now this word that I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people;
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,
Shew thy marvellous lovingkindness, O thou that savest by thy right hand them which put their trust in thee from those that rise up against them.
Oh how great is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee; which thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee before the sons of men!
And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, Lord, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.
That thy trust may be in the Lord, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.
But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
O continue thy lovingkindness unto them that know thee; and thy righteousness to the upright in heart.
Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until these calamities be overpast.
But mine eyes are unto thee, O God the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
He shall cover thee with his feathers, and under his wings shalt thou trust: his truth shall be thy shield and buckler.
From the wicked that oppress me, from my deadly enemies, who compass me about.
For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.
Their eyes stand out with fatness: they have more than heart could wish.
Therefore they sacrifice unto their net, and burn incense unto their drag; because by them their portion is fat, and their meat plenteous.
Their heart is as fat as grease; but I delight in thy law.
For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speak.
They have now compassed us in our steps: they have set their eyes bowing down to the earth;
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.

Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,
And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.
They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
And he went up and down among the lions, he became a young lion, and learned to catch the prey, and devoured men.
Their roaring shall be like a lion, they shall roar like young lions: yea, they shall roar, and lay hold of the prey, and shall carry it away safe, and none shall deliver it.
The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their meat from God.
Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,
Arise, O Lord, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:
Yet if thou warn the wicked, and he turn not from his wickedness, nor from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity; but thou hast delivered thy soul.
Seeing then that I will cut off from thee the righteous and the wicked, therefore shall my sword go forth out of his sheath against all flesh from the south to the north:
¶ Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will bring a sword upon thee, and cut off man and beast out of thee.
Arise, O Lord, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.

From men which are thy hand, O Lord, from men of the world, which have their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: they are full of children, and leave the rest of…
That thou givest them they gather: thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good.
Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.
And he hath cast the lot for them, and his hand hath divided it unto them by line: they shall possess it for ever, from generation to generation shall they dwell therein.
And wheresoever the children of men dwell, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the heaven hath he given into thine hand, and hath made thee ruler over them all. Thou art this head of gold.
As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness: I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.
In the high and glorious place. When shall I regain Thy presence. And again behold Thy face? In Thy holy habitation
I will praise thee with uprightness of heart, when I shall have learned thy righteous judgments.
The Lord make his face shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee:
For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.
Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
